name: Zayd ibn Ma'qil
title: Lion of Insight
shortDescription: A brave warrior and insightful Shia from Kufa who was martyred with unparalleled resistance during the first attack of Umar Sa'd's army.
fullStory:
Zayd ibn Ma'qil (or Badr ibn Ma'qil) was a sincere and renowned Shia from Kufa. Having complete insight and awareness of the situations of his time, upon hearing the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) arrival in the land of Karbala, he quickly prepared himself for battle and to help him.

Zayd, bypassing all obstacles and the strict controls of Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad's forces, secretly escaped Kufa and managed to join Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan. His presence in the Imam's camp indicated his deep faith and bravery.

On the morning of Ashura, when Umar Sa'd's 30,000-strong army, with the order to shoot arrows, began its cowardly attack on the tents of the Master of Martyrs (AS), Zayd was one of those who stood in the front line of defense with a drawn sword.

He stood firm against the rain of arrows and bravely charged into the heart of the enemy. In this attack, where many of the Imam's initial companions were martyred, Zayd also fought valiantly and brought down several Kufans.

Finally, this sincere soldier of Wilayah, after receiving numerous wounds and being hit by enemy arrows, sacrificed his sweet life for the righteousness of Imam Hussain (AS) and was placed among the martyrs of the first attack.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Zayd ibn Ma'qil.
